ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing Re Closing Of Estate
Off Calendar Jun-07-2017 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-19-2016 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jan-26-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Elizabeth Johnson for John Cullen. Beneficiaries Denise Grayson & Reginald Grayson in Propria Persona. John Contreras also present.

Ms. Johnson stated that Mr. Cullen represented Mr. Shea, that they had discussed estate planning, that options were offered and that Mr. Shea was very clear on who he wanted appointed and that was Mr. Cullen. Ms. Grayson stated that she is the domestic partner of Mr. Shea, that Mr. Cullen represented Mr. Shea on a disability case, that Mr. Cullen knew the Shea family well, that she filed documents on Friday, that questions remain unanswered, that she was told Mr. Cullen's fee would be $25,000.00, that the beneficiaries have a right to ask questions, that there is a written will, that Mr. Shea was on medication when he made the will, that Mr. Cullen has told her that he suggested to Mr. Shea that he and Ms. Grayson become domestic partners, that she has been told by Ms. Johnson that an accounting will be done, that she is raising two children and gave background on them, that they object to the fees that will be due to Mr. Cullen and Ms. Johnson, that she has obtained an attorney who has told her that Mr. Cullen could choose to bill hourly and provided the court with a copy of an invoice from Mr. Cullen to Mrs. Shea (Kevin's mother) which the court returned to her. Ms. Johnson stated that Ms. Grayson raising two children is irrelevant, that the property at issue is property Mr. Shea inherited from his mother, that Ms. Grayson has moved into the property, that Kevin Shea had no children, that Mr. Cullen was asked to be the executor and that he is not required to fill both roles. Mr. Reginald Grayson stated that they want his mother appointed as executor to save money to the estate. Ms. Grayson stated that $50,000.00 is a lot of money for a small estate and that they have been treated badly and not give information. Mr. Grayson stated that with communication this could have been settled and that Mr. Cullen is evasive. The court took the petition for probate of will and letters testamentary under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Dec-07-2015 Continued To Jan-25-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Denise Grayson, objector.

Ms. Grayson was present and stated that she wished to object to the granting of the petition. The Court stated that he would continue the matter and set January 15, 2016 as the deadline for her to file her objections or other response. The matter was continued to January 25, 2016 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Nov-23-2015 Continued To Dec-07-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
